title: 42 Wiki Compiler
description:
Compile any markdown wiki into an AI-agent-ready knowledge base.

**Quick start:** point at a public GitHub repo with .md files (or upload a zip), describe in 1-2 sentences what the wiki is for, get back a compiled wiki + MCP config snippet to paste into Claude Desktop / Cursor / Cline.

**3 pricing tiers:** BYOK Google ($0 + your Gemini costs), BYOK Anthropic ($0 + your Claude costs), or Hosted ($5/compile, uses our Gemini quota).

📚 [Documentation](https://github.com/42ROWS/wiki-embedded)

wikiSource.title: 📁 Wiki Source Type
wikiSource.description: Where to fetch the markdown files from.
wikiSource.sectionDescription: Provide the markdown wiki to compile. Files should be `.md` with optional YAML frontmatter. Wikilinks `[[slug]]` are auto-extracted as crossrefs.

githubRepo.title: 🐙 GitHub Repository
githubRepo.description: Format: `owner/repo` or `owner/repo/path/to/wiki/dir` (e.g. `karpathy/llm.c` or `mario/notes/wiki`). Public repos only.

githubBranch.title: 🌿 Branch
githubBranch.description: Branch to fetch from. Defaults to the repo's default branch.

zipUrl.title: 🔗 ZIP URL
zipUrl.description: Public URL to a `.zip` containing the wiki markdown files.

uploadedFiles.title: 📤 Upload Markdown Files
uploadedFiles.description: Upload `.md` files directly. Wikilinks `[[slug]]` between uploaded files are preserved.

thesisIntent.title: 🎯 Thesis Intent (1-2 sentences)
thesisIntent.description: Describe the purpose of the wiki and who will query it. The compiler uses this to derive a structured thesis (target audience, primary use case, answer style, key concepts, excluded topics). Example: 'A B2B sales rep doing account-based marketing for Italian PMI manufacturers, looking for prospects with high intent signals.'
thesisIntent.prefill: An AI agent answering practical questions about this knowledge base for its intended readers. Answers should be concise and cite the relevant wiki pages.
thesisIntent.sectionCaption: 🧠 Thesis (the lens through which the wiki is compiled)

answerStyle.title: ✍️ Preferred Answer Style
answerStyle.description: Hint for the chunk generation pass. Affects tone, depth, and citation style of the pre-computed chunks.
enumTitles: Tactical recipe / how-to (best for engineering wikis)

chunkArchetypes.title: 🧩 Chunk Archetypes
chunkArchetypes.description: Which categories of pre-computed answer chunks to generate. ~4 chunks per archetype, ~$0.01 each via Gemini Flash. Total cost scales with the number selected.

chunksPerArchetype.title: 🔢 Chunks per Archetype
chunksPerArchetype.description: Number of pre-computed answer chunks per archetype. More = better coverage but higher cost. Default 4 (good balance).

llmProvider.title: 🤖 LLM Provider
llmProvider.description: Which LLM to use for thesis derivation + chunk generation. BYOK = leave the matching key below. Hosted = leave keys empty (uses our Gemini quota, $5/compile).
enumTitles: 🧠 Anthropic Claude (BYOK — best quality on thesis)
llmProvider.sectionDescription: BYOK is free Apify-side, you pay the LLM provider directly (~$0.05-0.30 per wiki). Hosted is $5 flat per compile.

googleApiKey.title: 🔑 Google AI API Key (BYOK)
googleApiKey.description: Get yours at aistudio.google.com. Used for thesis (Gemini 2.5 Pro) + chunks (Gemini 2.5 Flash). Typical cost: $0.05-0.20 per wiki.

anthropicApiKey.title: 🔑 Anthropic API Key (BYOK)
anthropicApiKey.description: Get yours at console.anthropic.com. Used for thesis (Claude Sonnet) + chunks (Claude Haiku). Typical cost: $0.20-0.60 per wiki.

embeddingModel.title: 🔡 Embedding Model
embeddingModel.description: Pinecone Inference (cloud) is the default — 5M tokens/month free, ~30s for 137 pages. Local options use the actor's CPU (slower, no API key needed).
enumTitles: 💻 Local E5 multilingual-base (768d, ~500MB, no API key)
💻 Local E5 multilingual-large (1024d, ~2GB, no API key)
embeddingModel.sectionDescription: Cloud options require PINECONE_API_KEY env var on the actor. Local options run on the actor's CPU.

tenantId.title: 🏢 Tenant ID (Pinecone namespace)
tenantId.description: If set, the compiled vectors are synced to a Pinecone index (= the `collection`) under a namespace = this tenant id (e.g. the company id). The agent then retrieves from `(collection, tenantId)`. Leave empty to only produce the bundle (no Pinecone write). Requires PINECONE_API_KEY on the actor.
tenantId.sectionDescription: Write the vectors to a per-tenant Pinecone namespace so an agent can query them at runtime. collection → index, tenantId → namespace.

collection.title: 🗂️ Collection (Pinecone index)
collection.description: Which corpus this wiki belongs to (e.g. `company-wiki`, `marketing-skills`). Maps to a Pinecone index. Few collections (corpus types), many tenants (namespaces). Default: company-wiki.

priorBundleUrl.title: ♻️ Prior bundle URL (incremental update)
priorBundleUrl.description: Public URL of a previously compiled `compiled_wiki.zip` (e.g. the `compiled_wiki_url` from a prior run). When provided, the compiler diffs the new source against it and re-embeds / re-generates only what changed — much faster and cheaper. Leave empty for a full compile. Falls back to a full rebuild automatically if the embedding model changed or too much of the wiki changed.

ragDedup.title: ♻️ Skip near-duplicate chunks
ragDedup.description: Use Pinecone-backed RAG dedup: chunks with cosine similarity > 0.92 to existing ones are skipped. Saves LLM cost on refresh runs.

outputMcpManifest.title: 📦 Emit MCP Manifest
outputMcpManifest.description: Generate a plug-and-play MCP server config snippet (for Claude Desktop / Cursor / Cline) alongside the compiled wiki.
